segments.text: Welcome to lecture 4. In this lecture, we will discuss dataflow systems, which provide data management services for many applications without requiring coordination. We will explore how these systems can achieve better performance and fault tolerance than traditional systems that need synchronous coordination.

A dataflow system is a type of distributed system that provides data management services without requiring coordination. This means that the application code can focus on processing data, without worrying about the underlying data storage and retrieval mechanisms.

In a dataflow system, the application code responds to state changes by triggering state changes in another place. This implies that the relationship between application code and state management is renegotiated.

Dataflow systems can provide data management services without requiring coordination, while still giving strong integrity guarantees. This means that the system can achieve better performance and fault tolerance than traditional systems that need synchronous coordination.
